item.title: Exploring 🚀Python Data Types: A Quick Overview
item.description:
BALAKRISHNA S

Posted on Aug 29

 

  1. Integers (int):
    Examples: 42, -10, 0

  2. Floating-Point Numbers (float):
    Examples: 3.14, -0.5, 2.0

  3. Strings (str):
    Examples:'Hello, World!', "Python", '123'

  4. Lists (list):
    Examples:[1, 2, 3], ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']

  5. Tuples (tuple):
    Examples:(1, 2, 3), ('red', 'green', 'blue')

  6. Dictionaries (dict):
    Examples: {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30}, {'fruit': 'apple',
    'color': 'red'}

  7. Sets (set):
    Examples: {1, 2, 3}, {'apple', 'banana', 'cherry'}

  8. Booleans (bool):
    Examples: True, False

Understanding these data types helps us manage and manipulate data effectively in Python. Feel free to share your insights and experiences with these data types below! Let's discuss. 💬🚀

Once suspended, balakrishnasajja will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, balakrishnasajja will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by balakrishnasajja will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If balakrishnasajja is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag balakrishnasajja:

Make all posts by balakrishnasajja less visible

balakrishnasajja consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging balakrishnasajja will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: balakrishnasajja
